source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” normie+root should return Google-AI search results with live citations.
title: Research: grounded_search tool (Gemini Google-Search grounding + citations)
current_state: Gemini is used only as a plain text/tool provider; GoogleSearchRetrieval/google_search grounding is never instantiated. web_search returns raw snippets with no AI synthesis and no citations. There is no 'grounded answer with sources' path.
target_state: A grounded_search(query) tool returns a Gemini-synthesized answer grounded in LIVE Google Search, plus the grounding citations (source titles + URLs) from the response grounding metadata. Available to BOTH normie and root (add to normie tool_allowlist; root already has all). If Gemini is unavailable/exhausted (recall T-212 brownout), it falls back gracefully to web_search so the tool never hard-fails.
migration_plan: 1. core/providers/gemini.py: add a grounded generate path that attaches the google_search grounding tool (types.Tool(google_search=...) per the installed google-genai version) and extracts grounding_metadata (grounding_chunks/citations: title+uri) from the response. Keep the existing plain chat() untouched.

2. New tool grounded_search in tools_web.py (or a new tools_research.py): calls the grounded path, returns {answer, citations:[{title,url}], provider:'gemini-grounded'}. On any Gemini error, fall back to web_search and mark provider:'web_fallback'.
3. Register the ToolSpec; add 'grounded_search' to normie's tool_allowlist in agent/modes.py.
4. Cache + cost: reuse web_cache.db patterns where sensible; record tokens via the existing Gemini token accounting (T-212).

5. Tests: testing/test_grounded_search.py (offline â€” stub the genai client): returns answer + citations from mocked grounding metadata; falls back to web_search when the client raises; tool is present in normie's allowlist; citations are well-formed.
risk_notes: Grounding availability/quotas depend on the Gemini tier (we just fixed 2.0-flash brownout in T-212) â€” the web_search fallback is mandatory so a Gemini outage degrades gracefully instead of breaking research. Pin the grounding-tool construction to the actually-installed google-genai API shape (verify before relying on field names).
